rulings.Case Title: MORVEN LIBURD VS. BOSA DEVELOPMENT CALIFORNIA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 12, 2012, Line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT BOSA DEVELOPMENT CALIFORNIA II, INC. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. Cross-Complainant has stated a cause of action for breach of contract and the cause of action is not uncertain. Although Urata did not attach a copy of the alleged contract, it pled the alleged contract's legal intendment and effect, which is sufficient. Bosa's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. However, the Court notes that although it can take judicial notice of the existence of pleadings and other documents in the court record, the Court cannot accept as true the contents of pleadings or exhibits just because they are part of the court record. Day v. Sharp (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 904, 914. Therefore, the alleged contract between Quorum and Bosa that is relied on in the moving papers is not properly considered on this demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JEEBA ABBEYQUAYE VS. CHARLES BELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion To Compel Deft'S Further Responses To Form And Special Interrgoatrories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 12, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF JEEBA ABBEYQUAYE'S Motion To Compel Defendant's Further Responses To Form And Special Interrgoatrories, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Compel is granted is part and denied in part. As to Interrogatory Nos. 2.12, 2.13, 12.7, 15.1 and Special Interrogatory Nos. 1, 3, 4, 7, 13, 15 and 16, the motion is granted; however, Defendant is permitted to refer to documents pursuant to 2030.230; however, pursuant to that provision the reference to documents must be more specific. Requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARK S. BUZA VS. YAHOO! INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 12, 2012, Line 26. DEFENDANT YAHOO! INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action, overruled as to the third cause of action, and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action. Plaintiff has not lleged, nor could he, that defendant is a state actor subject to the free speech clause of the California Constitution. The third cause of action is adequately pled. The fourth cause of action is uncertain as to the phrase "intellectual property" and thus plaintiff is given leave to clarify that uncertainty. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted as to the federal district court decision and denied as to the remaining matters. Terms of conditions and a printout from a website are not proper subjects of judicial notice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
